posts.title: 📣 Pioneer Valley High School Wellness Center | May 2026
posts.summary: May is Mental Health Awareness Month, and the Wellness Center is excited to share a full calendar of activities, support, and connection opportunities for students. Monthly Highlights: Share & Care...
posts.author: Crystal Buchan
posts.body: May is Mental Health Awareness Month, and the Wellness Center is excited to share a full calendar of activities, support, and connection opportunities for students. Monthly Highlights: Share & Care Club Meetings - Held regularly (note: May 26 meeting cancelled). Peer Leaders Meeting - @ Lunch on May 6. Words of Encouragement Rock Painting - @ Lunch on May 7. Salty & Sweet DIY Salt Scrub Workshop - @ Lunch on May 14. Mental Health Awareness Week - May 18-22 Dress Up Day: Wear Green - May 20 to show support for mental health awareness. Cloud Dough Workshop - @ Lunch on May 18. Mental Health Resource Fair - @ Lunch on May 22. Mental Health Workshop (Hosted by CAPSLO) - @ Lunch on May 27. Important Dates & Closures: May 8: District Wellness Meeting @ 12:30 PM - Wellness Center Closed May 26: Wellness Center Closed (9:30 AM- 4:30 PM) May 25: Memorial Day - No School You are not alone. Join us this May as we prioritize well-being, connection, and support. Visit the Wellness Center to sign up for a workshop and get more details about May events. -Mrs. Buchan, Wellness Counselor -Ms. Collins, Wellness Coach

posts.title: 📬 PVHS Weekly Newsletter
posts.summary: WEEKLY UPDATE PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL May 1, 2026 Hello Panthers Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happenings and updates from ou...
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE

PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL

May 1, 2026

Hello Panthers

Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happenings and updates from our school community to keep you informed and engaged.
 

Paul R. Robinson, Ph.D. - Principal

Tiger Salamander Field Trip
Mr. Magni, Ms. Mihelic and Mrs. Zambrano took students on a field trip to a Tiger Salamander breeding facility. Students viewed the habitats and learned how scientists are helping support this sensitive population. Thank you teachers for providing such a wonderful educational experience for our students.
 
Superintendent Visit
Superintendent Garcia visited PVHS this week to meet with our Student Advisory Council.Mr. Garcia gathered input from our students on a possible update to our District cell phone policy. California's Assembly Bill 3216, the Phone-Free School Act, requires Districts to have a policy in place by June 2026. SMJUHSD currently has an “off and away” during instructional time policy, with the discretion for teachers to allow phones as needed for instruction. Mr. Garcia gathered input for an update to this policy and students discussed advantages and disadvantages of cell phone use on campus.
 
MWH DRS
Modern World History teachers engaged in a Data Reflection Session this week to discuss Cycle 4 test results. This engagement with student data helps teachers identify and expand our best practices. Thank you teachers for your hard work identifying how to best serve our students.
AVID 2027 Service Day
Ms. Wisz's AVID class hosted their community service project, Spring Family Fun Day, at Waller Park on Saturday. The event featured a variety of family friendly activities, including an Easter egg hunt, face painting, sponge and stencil painting, construction paper crafts, bracelet making, freeze tag, and soccer.
Snacks that the students and kids enjoyed were provided, and the event drew a strong turnout, allowing AVID students to offer kids and families a fun, no cost experience.
 

Merrie Okie-Goldin - Assistant Principal, Special Programs

Hello Panthers,
Riccardo Magni, Pioneer Valley High School’s AP Environmental Science teacher and Science Department Chair, was honored at the 2026 Earth Day celebration last Saturday at the Natural History Museum in Santa Maria for his decades of dedication to environmental education. With 30 years of teaching experience, Magni is known for his hands-on, field-based approach that students to engage deeply with the natural world. A recipient of multiple honors, including the Presidential Innovation Award and County Teacher of the Year. His impact extends beyond the classroom, as many of his students have gone on to pursue environmental careers and advocacy. Congratulation Riccardo!
Also, CAASPP testing will continue into next week for any students who have not yet completed their exams. Students who still need to finish will be called out of class to complete their testing. If a teacher needs to keep a student, we will coordinate to call them during another class period that allows for testing. Our goal is to reach 100% participation to meet state requirements, so we truly appreciate your support in reminding your student to come to school each day with a fully charged tablet.
We also want to extend a heartfelt thank you to our incredible staff for the dedication and effort poured into testing. A special thank you to our Department Chairs, TOSA's, ELPAC teachers, Math, Science, and English teachers for leading this work so diligently, and to team members Sasha Brafman, Jordyn MacCuish, Julie Swayze, Erica Dutton, Dawnette Cress and Amber Bernard for their support behind the scenes. We’re also grateful to Karine Perez, and to Cecilia Badley for coordinating substitutes and testing rooms. A special thank you to our community liaison, Flor Avila-Suarez, for stepping in alongside Special Education Secretary, Jessica Munoz, to help organize and call-in students who have been absent. Thank you to our Attendance team. Ben Alberry and Izzy Hernandez, you are smart cookies too! Thank you to Jenn Montanez and Scratch Cafe for the thank you treats for staff. Thank you to parents, guardians, and students for your attendance and support with participation.
This truly takes up a village, and we appreciate our admin team and admin assistants and everyone who has stepped up to support this big push. Thank you all for your help!
 

Jeffrey Hromadka - Assistant Principal, Student Services

🌟 A Massive Success at Future Panther Night!
What an incredible evening! This past Wednesday, the Panther Gym was buzzing with energy as we welcomed a huge crowd of prospective students and families for our Future Panther Night. The turnout was a testament to the vibrant community we are building here at Pioneer Valley High School.
🎭 Center Stage: Showcasing Our Talent
The night kicked off with a series of spectacular performances that showcased the diverse talent on our campus. We were treated to a captivating sneak peek of "Fiddler on the Roof" by our Drama department, vibrant cultural displays from Ballet Folklórico, and high-energy routines from the K-Pop Club. The FFA highlighted our strong agricultural roots, while the PVHS Drum Line provided an electrifying beat that kept the energy high all night long!
🤝 Finding Your "Crew"
After the performances, the floor transformed into a bustling marketplace of opportunity. Dozens of clubs and sports teams were on hand to meet with students.
We know that one of the greatest barometers of success for a high school student is being part of a group. Whether it’s on a field, in a lab, or on a stage, finding a sense of belonging is the first step toward a fulfilling four-year journey. Seeing so many future Panthers "shopping" for their extracurricular home was the highlight of the event!
❤️ A Heartfelt Thank You
This event would not have been possible without the dedication of our staff. Thank you to the teachers and coaches who stayed late to represent your programs. Your passion is infectious, and your encouragement is what gives these incoming students the confidence to take that first step toward success.
To our future Panthers: the energy you brought on Wednesday was amazing. We can’t wait to see you back on campus this fall!
 

Kevin Ilac - Assistant Principal, Instruction

This week was Sexual Assault Awareness Week. The Wellness Center, in partnership with the SHARe & Care Club, hosted multiple awareness activities during lunch. These activities helped raise awareness, promote consent, and remind our school community that support and resources are available.
  
On Thursday, the C & I Team held an AHC Registration Rally. Over 130 seniors completed the last step of their Allan Hancock Promise and registered for their Fall classes. Students that completed all of their steps were awarded priority registration at 9:00 am on Thursday. 
 
  Friday, 78 seniors who committed to a four-year university were celebrated with a signing day ceremony at lunch. May 1st is traditionally known as "Signing Day" in the United States. Students were recognized for their amazing achievement in the amphitheater. 
 

Christian Basulto - Dean of Students

Panther of the Quarter
This Tuesday, we proudly celebrated our Panther of the Quarter winners. Students and staff who have gone above and beyond to demonstrate the values of Panther PRIDE were recognized. The event was a huge success and a true reflection of the positive energy on our campus. A big thank you goes out to Mr. Dickinson’s Floral students for creating the beautiful flower arrangements and decorating the tables, which added such a warm and elegant touch to the celebration. 
Congratulations to our student winners: (9th grade) Julissa Alvarenga Torres, Frankie Hernandez, Danna Jacobo Rivera, and Jordan Nunez Nevarez; (10th grade) Brenda Antonio Santos, Brigida Calleja Mentado, Camille Gutierrez, and Alex Vargas-Vasquez; (11th grade) Ethyn Enriquez, Aubrey Johnson, Kiera Leon, and Luis Reyes-Velasco; and (12th grade) Jeffrey "JJ" Brown, Angel Daniel Camacho, Kevin Montes, and Paula Novela Rodrigeuz. These students were nominated for consistently showing leadership, responsibility, and care for others both inside and outside the classroom.
We also honored four outstanding staff members for their hard work and dedication. Congratulations to certificated staff Allison Pehle and Regina Orosco, and classified staff Andres Cardenas and Leticia Gamino! Thank you to everyone who helped us recognize the amazing people who help make PVHS such a special place.
 
PVHS Boys' Volleyball
This Thursday, our PVHS Boys Volleyball team competed against Mission Prep for the Ocean League Championship, and they delivered! The Panthers battled hard and came away with a 3-1 victory to secure the league title.
The fans in attendance were in for a treat. Set scores were 25-17, 27-25, 22-25, and the team closed out the match with an exciting 30-28 win in the fourth set!! 
Congratulations to our players and coaches on an incredible season and well-earned league championship. Best of luck as they move on to compete in the CIF playoffs!
Go Panthers! 🐾🏐
 
  PVHS WEBSITE

posts.title: 🎪 Final Reminder: Future Panther Night is Tomorrow (4/29 Wednesday)
posts.summary: FINAL REMINDER: Future Panther Night is TOMORROW (Wed 4/29)! (Note Time Change) We are officially less than 24 hours away from welcoming our newest Panthers!
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
FINAL REMINDER: Future Panther Night is TOMORROW (Wed 4/29)! (Note Time Change)

We are officially less than 24 hours away from welcoming our newest Panthers! Please take note of a slight update to our schedule to ensure you don’t miss our exciting opening ceremonies.

⏰ Updated Schedule:

  • 5:30 PM: Doors Open (Panther Gym)
  • 5:45 PM:Live Performances Begin!
Make sure to arrive by 5:30 PM to grab a seat before our student performers take the floor to kick off the night with a bang!

🎒 What’s in Store for You?

Once the performances conclude, the floor is yours! This is the ultimate "one-stop shop" for everything Pioneer Valley:
  • Club & Team Showcase: From the soccer field to the robotics lab, come "shop" for the activities that make high school memorable.
  • Meet Your Coaches & Teachers: Get face-to-face time with the mentors who will support your student over the next four years.
  • Essential Info: Get the final word on what you need for a successful transition this fall.

📍 Location Recap

Where: The Panther Gym When: Wednesday, April 29th @ 5:30 PM- 7:00PM We’ve put in the work to make this a fun, engaging, and informative night for the whole family. We can't wait to see the gym filled with the energy of our future Class of 2030! See you tomorrow night! Go Panthers! 🐾

posts.title: 📝 CAASPP Testing Begins Tomorrow – Juniors (ELA & Math)
posts.summary: 📢 CAASPP Testing Begins Tomorrow – Juniors (ELA & Math) Dear Students, Staff, and Families, Tomorrow, our junior students will begin the California Assessment of Student Performance and Progress (...
posts.author: Merrie Okie
posts.body:
📢 CAASPP Testing Begins Tomorrow – Juniors (ELA & Math) Dear Students, Staff, and Families, Tomorrow, our junior students will begin the California Assessment of Student Performance and Progress (CAASPP) testing in English Language Arts and Mathematics. The CAASPP system includes the Smarter Balanced Assessments (SBAC), which measure what students know and are able to do based on California’s academic standards in reading, writing, and math. These assessments are an important statewide requirement and provide valuable information about student learning, progress, and readiness for college and careers. This is a state-mandated assessment, and it is critical that we have all students present and participating so we meet the required participation rate. ✅ What we need from families:
  • Please ensure your student is present, on time, and ready to test
  • Encourage a good night’s sleep and a charged device 
  • Reinforce the importance of doing their best
Our goal is to achieve 100% participation, and your support makes a big difference. Testing will continue over the next couple of weeks, so consistent attendance is essential during this time. If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out via email or phone call. Thank you for your partnership and support! Merrie Goldin  Assistant Principal over Special Programs 805-922-1305 Ext. 5705

posts.title: 🎪 Future Panther Night
posts.summary: ⚡ Don't Miss Out: Discover the Panther Experience! We are just days away!
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
⚡ Don't Miss Out: Discover the Panther Experience!

We are just days away! If you are a prospective student or parent that has a student joining Pioneer Valley High School family this fall, our Future Panther Night is the one event you don't want to miss.

📍 When & Where

• Wednesday, April 29th

• 5:00 PM – 7:00 PM

• Location: The Panther Gym

🎨 Beyond the Classroom: Shop Your Future!

High school is about more than just textbooks—it’s about finding your passion and your "crew." We’ve designed this night to be interactive and engaging for students:

• Club & Sports Fair: Students can "shop" through dozens of extracurricular activities. Whether it's joining a sports team or finding a niche hobby in a student club, this is where the fun begins.

• Talk to the Pros: Coaches and teachers will be available at their booths to chat one-on-one about what makes PVHS such an exciting place to learn and grow.

• Ask a Panther: Get the inside scoop from current staff and leaders about how to make the most of the next four years.

📚 Parent Peace of Mind

While the students explore, parents will receive all the essential information needed to ensure a smooth transition in August. We’ll cover everything from campus culture to the resources available to help your student thrive.

Come see what makes being a Panther so special. We can’t wait to welcome the Class of 2030!

posts.title: 📣 Sexual Assault Awareness Week | Wellness Center Activities & Resources
posts.summary: The Wellness Center, in partnership with the SHARe & Care Club, is hosting Sexual Assault Awareness Week this week. This is a time to raise awareness, promote consent, and remind our school communi...
posts.author: Crystal Buchan
posts.body: The Wellness Center, in partnership with the SHARe & Care Club, is hosting Sexual Assault Awareness Week this week. This is a time to raise awareness, promote consent, and remind our school community that support and resources are available. Please see the attached flyer for our daily dress‑up themes and on‑campus activities, including: Monday (4/27): Wear Teal, the color for Sexual Assault Awareness - Information tabling at lunch in front of the Wellness Center Tuesday (4/28): Consent is Black & White- wear Black & White Wednesday (4/29): Denim Day Rally - Tabling at the Amphitheater with a K‑POP performance and the North County Rape Crisis Center Thursday (4/30): Support Survivors Day - Sign the banner in the Wellness Center with messages of encouragement Friday (5/1): Wear Flannel - Highlighting that anyone can be affected by sexual assault, regardless of background or appearance Throughout the week, students are welcome to visit the Wellness Center to participate in activities, ask questions, or speak privately with a trusted adult. Support & Resources If you or someone you know needs support, help is available: Local Resources: North County Rape Crisis & Child Protection Center (serving Santa Maria & Santa Barbara County) 24‑Hour Hotline: (805) 564‑3696 https://www.ncrcc.org<https://www.ncrcc.org/> National Resources: National Sexual Assault Hotline (RAINN): 800‑656‑HOPE (4673) https://www.rainn.org<https://www.rainn.org/> (Confidential support via phone or chat, 24/7) 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline: Call or text 988 for emotional support, 24/7 Participation is optional, and all students are encouraged to engage at a level that feels comfortable for them. We appreciate your support in helping create a safe, respectful, and caring school environment. If you have questions or would like additional support, please visit or contact Mrs. Buchan, Wellness Counselor or Ms. Collins, Certified Wellness Coach.
